desc: Dubai is a high-energy Gulf hub mixing iconic skyline attractions, beaches, shopping, and resort infrastructure with strong winter-season demand. November-March is the core comfort and peak-demand window, while late spring through early autumn is much hotter but can bring significant accommodation value outside holiday periods.
hls: Landmark skyline and mega-attraction city breaks
Excellent winter sun accessibility from Europe
Wide range from luxury resorts to budget hotels
Strong family entertainment and dining breadth
Beach, desert, and urban experiences in one destination

todo.name: Burj Khalifa and Downtown district
todo.desc: Visit observation decks and surrounding promenades in Dubai's core skyline zone.

todo.name: Old Dubai souks and creek
todo.desc: Explore spice/gold souks, traditional quarters, and short creek crossings.

todo.name: Desert safari evening
todo.desc: Take a licensed operator trip for dunes, sunset stops, and camp-style dining.

todo.name: Dubai Marina and JBR walk
todo.desc: Use waterfront promenades for evening dining and skyline views.

todo.name: Beach day at Kite or La Mer area
todo.desc: Pair city sightseeing with a beach segment in cooler months.

todo.name: Day trip to Abu Dhabi
todo.desc: Visit Sheikh Zayed Grand Mosque and Louvre Abu Dhabi on a full-day route.

wifi.note: Very strong hotel and apartment connectivity across most business and leisure districts.

prac.fltNote: Direct flights are available from Gatwick; London City typically requires a connection. Typical return fares are around £280-£450 in hotter off-peak periods, £450-£700 in shoulder demand, and £700+ in Christmas/New Year and prime winter-sun windows.
prac.visa: The UAE grants some nationalities visa-free or visa-on-arrival access, while others need a pre-arranged visa; rules can differ by passport and length of stay.
alerts: Heat and humidity are intense from May to September; plan outdoor activity timing carefully.
Major trade fairs and holiday periods can sharply increase hotel and flight pricing.
Transport times rise during peak commuting windows despite strong road and metro networks.

sweet: November-March is best for outdoor comfort; April and October can offer good value between peak winter demand and extreme summer heat.
